lead_signal.headline: Turkey's ongoing institutional capture under Erdogan
lead_signal.summary: Turkey continues steady democratic decline under President Erdogan and AKP, marked by unfair elections, weaponized institutions, and media suppression. Prominent opposition leader Ekrem Imamoglu arrested in March 2025 on corruption and terrorism charges. Freedom House's March 2026 report awards Turkey the worst due process score globally and rates it 'Not Free'.

erosion_developments.title: Erdogan's sustained backsliding via elections, judiciary, media
erosion_developments.summary: Turkey has experienced over two decades of democratic decline under Erdogan and AKP, including unfair elections with fraud claims since 2014, weaponization of institutions like special courts undermining judicial independence, and media suppression with 137 press freedom violations in 2025. Opposition leader Ekrem Imamoglu arrested March 2025; 14 CHP mayors and 200 officials imprisoned. European Commission 2025 report notes worsening politicization of judiciary; Freedom House March 2026 report gives worst global due process score.

sources_cited.title: Democratic Erosion in Turkey: Elections, Institutions, and Media Under Erdogan

institutional_events.event: Government intervention in cases, special courts undermining independence; 14 CHP mayors imprisoned while AKP allies unpunished
institutional_events.significance: Transforms state institutions into tools of political control, eroding judicial independence and rule of law as per European Commission 2025 report

actor_tracker.change_this_week: Continued documentation of institutional weaponization and opposition repression, including post-2025 arrests
actor_tracker.significance: Consolidates power through coordinated erosion of guardrails, shifting regime to competitive authoritarianism

weekly_brief_narrative: Turkey's democratic erosion under President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and the Justice and Development Party (AKP) persists as the week's primary signal, with a Democratic Erosion Consortium analysis published April 17 detailing over two decades of backsliding. What happened: Ekrem Imamoglu, prominent opposition leader and Istanbul mayor, was arrested in March 2025 on corruption and terrorism charges, part of a pattern imprisoning 14 CHP mayors and over 200 officials while AKP allies face no similar accountability; unfair elections since 2014 feature fraud claims, computerized voter rolls lacking transparency, and AKP exploitation of campaigning rules; media saw 137 violations in 2025 per Media Freedom Rapid Response, ranking Turkey 158/180; judicial independence suffers from government interventions in cases and special courts, as noted in European Commission’s 2025 report. Institutional effect: These measures weaponize rule of law, politicize judiciary, and capture institutions, transforming them from constraints to tools of control; Freedom House’s March 2026 report assigns Turkey the world’s worst due process score and ‘Not Free’ status, downgrading it to competitive authoritarianism. Pattern significance: Erdogan’s approach exemplifies gradual autocratisation via coordinated raises in dissent costs, institutional capture, and critical voice silencing, without dramatic ruptures; V-Dem Dataset v16 (through 2025) would likely flag active autocratisation given continued decline, though exact LDI unavailable here—consistent with global freedom decline for 20th year per Freedom House March 19, 2026 release. Adjacent signals: No direct electoral events in next 90 days, but narrowed democratic correction window with rival disqualified and media suppressed; potential SCEM relevance if opposition repression escalates unrest, though no 7-day triggers; aligns with Tier 5 analytical frames from Democratic Erosion Consortium on slow erosion dynamics.
